title: erigeron salishii
intro: Erigeron salishii, also known as Squaw's Hair or Salish's Fleabane, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a member of the daisy family and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, and are said to have antioxidant and immunostimulatory effects. Erigeron salishii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and immune function.
